auth_notes: Requires creating an application in Azure Portal (Microsoft Entra ID) or Google Cloud Console. Users must configure Redirect URIs, select specific granular scopes, and potentially obtain Admin Consent for organization-wide access.
rate_limits: Microsoft Graph: Service-specific (e.g., 10,000 requests per 10 minutes); Google: Quota-based (e.g., 100s of requests per second depending on the specific API).
gotchas: Microsoft Graph requires a lifecycle management strategy for webhooks as subscriptions expire every few days.
Google APIs often require domain-wide delegation for service accounts to act on behalf of users.
Handling pagination requires manual logic to follow '@odata.nextLink' or 'nextPageToken' in the HTTP Request node.
research_notes: To integrate without native nodes, use the HTTP Request node with n8n's 'OAuth2' credential type. For Office 365, target the Microsoft Graph API (graph.microsoft.com). For Google, target the specific REST API endpoint (e.g., www.googleapis.com/drive/v3). Webhook implementation will require a separate workflow to handle the initial validation handshake (e.g., returning a plain text validation token for MS Graph).
